title: Beadlea pringlei
intro: Beadlea pringlei, also known as Mexican beadle, is a plant species within the family Salicaceae, native to Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing fever. The bioactive constituents of Beadlelea pringlei include salicylic acid, tannins, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Beadlelea pringlei is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, often in combination with other plants to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Beadlea pringlei is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It is native to Mexico.
